title: Web Development Basics
description: Test your knowledge of core HTML, CSS, and JavaScript concepts.

questions.question: What does HTML stand for?
questions.explanation: HTML stands for Hyper Text Markup Language. It is the standard markup language for documents designed to be displayed in a web browser.

questions.question: Which CSS property controls the text size?
questions.explanation: The font-size property is used to change the size of the text in CSS.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a JavaScript data type?
questions.explanation: JavaScript has standard data types like Number, String, Boolean, Undefined, but it treats all numbers as 'Number' (64-bit floating point), so 'Float' is not a distinct data type in JS.

questions.question: What does CSS stand for?
questions.explanation: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets. It describes how HTML elements are to be displayed on screen, paper, or in other media.

questions.question: Inside which HTML element do we put the JavaScript?
questions.explanation: The <script> tag is used to embed a client-side script (JavaScript) in an HTML document.
